architecture.value: aarch64 (RPM naming convention shows ham-libs-0.0.1.aarch64.rpm); verified on x86_64 also succeeds

os_selection.source: README.md - RPM版本/代码仓信息; User_Guide.md

value: libboundscheck (git submodule, must be built and installed to /usr/lib64/libboundscheck.so)
dos2unix (for mockcpp patching in test script)

build_commands.value: git submodule update --init --recursive && mkdir build && cd build && cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release .. && make install -j<N>  OR  bash build.sh -t release

ut_commands.value: bash test/run_dt.sh (initializes submodules, builds libboundscheck, gtest, mockcpp, compiles ham_dt, runs gtest)
ut_commands.source: README.md - Run test suite section + test/run_dt.sh

sample_commands.value: No standalone sample executables. HAM produces libham.so shared library only. Usage example code shown in doc/Developer_Tutorial.md via dlopen.

process_timeline.action: Attempted Ubuntu 24.04 pull, failed on DNS. Switched to openEuler 22.03
Installed cmake, gcc, g++, make, git, dos2unix via yum
Ran cmake configuration for Release build
First make install attempt - failed due to missing libboundscheck.so
Built libboundscheck from 3rdparty submodule and installed to /usr/lib64/
Ran test/run_dt.sh - all 8 tests passed, lcov coverage skipped
Generated JSON report and Markdown session summary

ut.note: Test binary ham_dt compiled and executed successfully. lcov coverage report generation skipped (lcov not available).

sample.note: HAM is a shared library (libham.so). No standalone sample executables. Usage examples are documented in doc/Developer_Tutorial.md as C code using dlopen.

documentation_gaps: README.md states 'get the dependent header files of libboundscheck' but does not mention that libboundscheck.so must be compiled and installed to /usr/lib64/. The build will fail without this step.
build.sh uses 'make install -j ${CPU_PROCESSOR_NUM}' without any concurrency limiting. On resource-constrained systems this could cause issues.
README.md mentions 'bash build.sh' without '-t release' or '-t debug' parameter, but the script requires -t flag (usage shows valid options: debug, release, clean).
test/run_dt.sh depends on lcov and genhtml for coverage reports, but these are not listed as prerequisites. Coverage generation will fail silently if not installed.
No sample/example executable provided in the repository - only library source. Users must write their own dlopen-based integration code per Developer_Tutorial.md.
Docker/CI instructions are absent. No Dockerfile or container-based build instructions provided.

problems_encountered.problem: Docker pull ubuntu:24.04 failed - DNS resolution error for internal-registry.example.com
problems_encountered.solution: Switched to locally available openEuler 22.03 image (internal-registry.example.com/openeuler/openeuler:22.03).
